spec_match.rationale: Uses the event to inspect clipboard contents and proactively show contextual paste UI — exactly the Notion/Figma pattern the chromestatus motivation describes.

interactive.rationale: Seeded copy buttons, a contenteditable target, a toast that actually inserts text/links/images on click.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a Notion-style paste hint that fires on copy; the page demonstrates that with three content types.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers text, URL, image, and has a permission-error block, but doesn't handle the 'user copies sensitive data and then immediately copies something else' debounce path, nor a way to dismiss + suppress for the session.

design_system.rationale: Variables only. The toast button overrides background to --accent-emerald and color to --bg-ivory together — the invariant 6 trap is avoided.

openQuestions.title: Surface the privacy / focus rules
openQuestions.detail: The page mentions in prose that the event fires regardless of focus but the read requires permission. Make that real — add a 'unfocus the page and copy something elsewhere' instruction and a counter showing how many events arrived while the page was blurred. That's the new behaviour that chromestatus highlights as the motivation.

openQuestions.title: Add a debounce / coalesce demo
openQuestions.detail: Rapid-fire copies should not flash three toasts. Right now the toast just overwrites itself. Add a debounce control and show how the UX changes when copies arrive within 200 ms of each other.

openQuestions.title: Heuristic for distinguishing 'paste as link' vs 'paste as text'
openQuestions.detail: The current detector only checks `/^https?:\/\//`. It misses `mailto:`, schemeless URLs, and oEmbed-detectable URLs (YouTube, Twitter). The page would teach the heuristic better if it had a slider/switch for 'strict / liberal URL detection' and showed real-world cases.

summary: Genuinely useful pattern demo that captures Notion/Figma-style proactive UX. Lean on the off-focus story to show what this event uniquely enables (vs paste-event listeners) and tighten the URL heuristic.
